Object 100 - Supercluster Servers

[[File:Frontier 02-1024x733.png|link=|center|425x425px]]

'''Object 100''', also known as "'''Supercluster Servers'''," consists of an undefined set of servers connected to a distributed cloud network of metadata underlying the [[Phenomenon 2|Back-Net]]. These servers access information that describes the entirety of the current and diverse source code utilized across Back-Net and are responsible for the constant functioning of this source code The servers are highly indestructible, tests prove that the structure of the servers appears not to be affected by energy transfer, nullifying joule interactions. Furthermore, it is theorized that servers operate independently of electrical power, being completely autonomous The servers operate with a performance metric based on an unknown computation surpassing the exascale, capable of performing consecutive calculations for code interpretation. It is theorized that these servers function as "background feeders," autonomously interpreting code through continuous processes that update the Database with new pages and codes being constantly analyzed, allowing every Terminals to operate Tests using a supercomputer, developed by [[The Kauer Research Organization]] and [[M.E.G.]], in conjunction with the with the servers’ infrastructure, demonstrated that entire levels could be visualized in a meta-informational state when searched within the cloud. However, most communications between computers and servers result solely in the visualization of these codes, as if the cloud code were blocked from any type of change or modification It is theorized that the metadata complex within the cloud hosted by these servers is responsible for the anomalous properties of [[Entity PC|The End's Computer]], with the computer serving as a cloud management console interface. Because through the computer it is possible to modify the source code of the metadata contained in the cloud, not only changing the structure of the modified page in the Back-net, but also the level or subject to which the page is related This metadata is believed to describe not only the data on the Back-net but also their real-world counterparts. It acts as a superior informational metadata layer, wherein both the page of a level is viewed as code, and the level itself in reality is also subject to this coding structure being processed. With the servers served not just as background feeders for Back-net, but for reality itself {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''Here where reality is processed?'''|final='''Yes.'''|body= '''Author:''' [[User:Roger_D._Tech|Roger D. Tech]] {{User:Roger D. Tech/pages}} }}
